page: Microsoft Azure L32as_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the LASv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 7763 processor featuring 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a substantial local storage capacity of 8008 GB that is Premium Storage capable. No graphics processing units are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results show strong performance in single-core CPU execution, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2847.38 and a PassMark memory score of 2852.32, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Multi-core CPU performance is average, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 43243.72 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 14326.0. Memory bandwidth is also moderate, with read-only large block bandwidth at 79.55 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 45.38 GB/sec. Image processing represents another area of strength, scoring 14374.0 on Geekbench.

While the instance handles small and medium language model inference at average speeds, it exhibits weak performance on large-scale workloads, processing only 6.58 tokens per second during prompt processing for a 70B parameter model. The high local storage capacity of 8008 GB combined with a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive deployments that require high resource density without the premium cost of dedicated GPU hardware. This server is suited for database operations, memory-intensive applications, large-scale web serving, and local storage-heavy workloads.
description: The Microsoft Azure L32as_v3 is an x86_64 server instance from the LASv3 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM and 8008 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage, with no GPU. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. However, it exhibits weak performance in large-scale LLM inference, processing only 6.58 tokens per second on a 70B model. This high-density storage and memory configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive workloads. It is suited for database hosting, memory-heavy applications, and local storage-intensive tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L32as_v3 is an x86_64 server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 8008 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for database and storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L32as_v3 is an x86_64 server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 8008 GB local storage, optimized for database and memory-heavy tasks.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors for data-intensive workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
256.0 GB of system memory paired with 8008 GB of local storage
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Weak performance on large-scale 70B parameter LLM inference
Suited for database hosting, web serving, and storage-heavy applications
